description: YouTube Comment Analyzer - Collect the latest 100 comments from a YouTube video and analyze them with an LLM to help creators understand audience sentiment and actionable feedback. No server, no login required.

apis: YouTube Data API v3 (key read from YOUTUBE_API_KEY env var via import.meta.env)
OpenAI API via openai npm package (key read from OPENAI_API_KEY env var via import.meta.env)
techStack.deployment: static files only — runs entirely in the browser

userStories.title: Project setup and URL input UI
userStories.description: As a YouTuber, I want a clean web page where I can enter a YouTube video URL and my API keys so I can start analyzing comments.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize Vite + TypeScript project (npm create vite)
Input field for YouTube video URL (supports youtube.com/watch?v= and youtu.be/ formats)
Read YOUTUBE_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY from import.meta.env (set via .env.local)
Show clear error if either env var is missing
Analyze button that triggers the comment collection flow
Basic responsive CSS layout — clean and readable
npm run build and typecheck pass

userStories.title: Fetch latest 100 YouTube comments
userStories.description: As a YouTuber, I want the app to collect the 100 most recent comments from my video so they can be analyzed.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract video ID from YouTube URL (handle both youtube.com and youtu.be formats)
Call YouTube Data API v3 commentThreads.list to fetch up to 100 top-level comments
Store comment text, like count, and author name for each comment
Show a loading spinner while fetching
Show a user-friendly error if the API key is invalid, quota is exceeded, or the video is not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sentiment analysis — positive / negative / neutral
userStories.description: As a YouTuber, I want each comment classified by sentiment so I can see the overall mood of my audience.
acceptanceCriteria: Send all collected comments to OpenAI API in a single batch prompt
Each comment classified as 'positive', 'negative', or 'neutral'
Display sentiment breakdown: count and percentage for each label
Three summary stat cards styled with matching colors: green (positive), red (negative), gray (neutral)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract top praise, advice, and criticism comments
userStories.description: As a YouTuber, I want to see the most notable comments grouped into praise, advice, and criticism so I know exactly what viewers are saying.
acceptanceCriteria: From comments sorted by like count (descending), use OpenAI gpt-4.1-nano to classify each as 'praise', 'advice', 'criticism', or 'other'
Display top 5 comments per category (praise / advice / criticism) with comment text and like count
Each category rendered in its own clearly labeled section
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Overall improvement summary
userStories.description: As a YouTuber, I want a concise AI-generated summary of my strengths and areas to improve so I can act on the feedback quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenAI gpt-4.1-nano generates 3–5 bullet points of 'what you're doing well' based on comment themes
OpenAI gpt-4.1-nano generates 3–5 bullet points of 'areas to improve' based on comment themes
Summary section displayed at the top of results, above the sentiment stats
Typecheck passes
